title: Oreogrammitis attenuata
intro: Oreogrammitis attenuata, also known as New Guinea fern, is a species of fern native to the tropical regions of the Pacific Islands. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce inflammation and protect against cell damage. The bioactive constituents of Oreogrammitis attenuata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and coumarins, which are believed to contribute to its therapeutic effects. The most common herbal preparations made with Oreogrammitis attenuata include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to support immune function and promote overall well-being.

Oreogrammitis attenuata is a specific genus within the family Polypodiaceae, and it is native to New Caledonia.
